executive_summary.description: A high-level overview of the City of Richmond's procurement and contract management systems as of 2025-2026, summarizing the key technologies used, data availability, and answers to the user's primary questions.

system_acronym.description: The acronym for the city's ERP system.

system_full_name.description: The full name of the ERP system, 'RVA: Advancing Proven Innovative Direction System'.

technology_platform.description: The underlying technology of the ERP system, which is Oracle E-Business Suite.

system_type.description: The type of system, identified as an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system.

platform_name.description: The name of the e-procurement platform used for solicitations, which is OpenGov.

portal_url.description: The direct URL to the City of Richmond's procurement portal on OpenGov.

primary_function.description: The main purpose of the platform, such as for electronic submission of bids and proposals.

primary_system.description: The name of the document management system used for storing contracts, identified as OnBase.

storage_method.description: Describes how contracts are stored, such as in a centralized document management system.

integration_details.description: Information on how this system integrates with other city systems, such as the RAPIDS ERP.

portal_name.description: The name of the vendor portal, which is the Oracle iSupplier portal.

portal_url.description: The URL for vendors to register or log in to the portal.

key_features.description: A list of functions available to vendors through the portal, such as registration, viewing opportunities, and managing account information.

dataset_name.description: The official name of the dataset, e.g., 'City Contracts' or 'Payment Register'.

dataset_id.description: The unique identifier for the dataset on the Socrata portal, e.g., 'xqn7-jvv2'.

fields.description: A list of the data columns or fields available within the dataset.

row_count.description: The approximate number of rows in the dataset at the time of the query.

last_updated_date.description: The date the dataset was last updated.

city_open_data_procurement_datasets.description: A list of procurement-related datasets published on the City of Richmond's Socrata Open Data Portal. Each item should detail specific dataset information including name, dataset ID, data fields/columns, approximate row count, and the last update date, with a focus on the 'City Contracts' (xqn7-jvv2) and 'Payment Register' datasets.

system_name.description: The name of the statewide system, eVA (electronic Virginia).

description.description: A brief description of the eVA system and its purpose.

data_availability.description: Information on where and how procurement data from eVA can be accessed, e.g., Virginia Open Data Portal.

api_availability.description: The status of whether a public API is available for accessing eVA data.

system_name.description: The name of the system for state IT contracts, VITA CobblestoneContracts.

technology_provider.description: The software vendor that powers the system, Cobblestone Systems.

public_portal_url.description: The URL to the public-facing portal for searching IT contracts.

api_availability.description: The status of whether a public API is available for the VITA contract system.

vendor_name.description: The name of the procurement software vendor, e.g., Oracle, OpenGov, Jaggaer.

relationship_status.description: The status of the city's relationship with the vendor (e.g., 'Confirmed', 'No Evidence Found').

details.description: Specific details about the nature of the relationship, such as the products or services used.

summary_of_findings.description: A summary of the information found regarding the city's IT procurement and security review process.

ato_process_confirmed.description: A boolean indicating whether evidence of a formal Authority to Operate (ATO) process was found.

system_interconnectivity_overview.description: A synthesized explanation of how the various systems (OpenGov, RAPIDS, iSupplier, OnBase) likely interconnect and function together throughout the city's procurement lifecycle, from solicitation to payment.
